test_output_summary: 12/12 tests pass in tests/test_stub_capabilities.py covering all four AC. Combined run with tests/test_config.py: 57 passed, 0 failed.
implementation_notes: Created src/jarvis/config/stub_capabilities.yaml by copying the canonical YAML block from docs/design/FEAT-JARVIS-002/models/DM-stub-registry.md §'Canonical Phase 2 content' verbatim. The file declares version: "1.0" plus four capability entries — architect-agent (specialist), product-owner-agent (specialist), ideation-agent (specialist), and forge (core). All agent_id values are kebab-case, all tool_name values are snake_case, all trust_tier values come from the {core, specialist, extension} set. The forge entry exposes a build_feature capability so the reasoning-model catalogue surfaces Forge alongside the specialist agents (per DDR-008 + DM-stub-registry §'Rationale for the fourth entry'). Wrote tests/test_stub_capabilities.py with class-based AC organisation per .claude/rules/testing.md. AC-002 is verified by extracting the canonical YAML block from DM-stub-registry.md at test time and asserting byte-for-byte equality with the file on disk — both are 3271 bytes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] File exists at `src/jarvis/config/stub_capabilities.yaml` containing exactly four capabilities: `architect-agent`, `product-owner-agent`, `ideation-agent`, `forge`.
completion_promises.evidence: Created src/jarvis/config/stub_capabilities.yaml. tests/test_stub_capabilities.py::TestAC001FileExistsWithFourCapabilities asserts the file exists at that path, parses to a mapping with exactly four entries under `capabilities`, and that the agent_ids list equals ['architect-agent', 'product-owner-agent', 'ideation-agent', 'forge'].

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Content matches byte-for-byte the canonical YAML in DM-stub-registry.md §"Canonical Phase 2 content".
completion_promises.evidence: tests/test_stub_capabilities.py::TestAC002CanonicalByteForByteMatch::test_byte_equal_to_canonical extracts the YAML block bracketed by ```yaml … ``` immediately following the 'Canonical Phase 2 content' heading in docs/design/FEAT-JARVIS-002/models/DM-stub-registry.md and asserts equality with the file on disk. Both are 3271 UTF-8 bytes (verified with len(...).encode('utf-8')); the test passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] All `agent_id` values are kebab-case; all `tool_name` values are snake_case; all `trust_tier` values are one of `core|specialist|extension`.
completion_promises.evidence: tests/test_stub_capabilities.py::TestAC003NamingAndTrustTier validates every agent_id against the kebab-case regex `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$`, every tool_name against the snake_case regex `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(?:_[a-z0-9]+)*$`, and every trust_tier against the set {'core', 'specialist', 'extension'}. All four agent_ids (architect-agent, product-owner-agent, ideation-agent, forge), all seven tool_names (run_architecture_session, draft_adr, review_specification, refine_acceptance_criteria, generate_alternatives, steelman, build_feature) and all four trust_tiers (specialist, specialist, specialist, core) pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `forge` entry carries a `build_feature` capability so the reasoning model sees Forge alongside specialists in the catalogue.
completion_promises.evidence: tests/test_stub_capabilities.py::TestAC004ForgeBuildFeatureCapability::test_forge_has_build_feature_capability locates the entry with agent_id == 'forge' and asserts 'build_feature' is in [cap['tool_name'] for cap in forge['capability_list']]. The forge entry's tool exposes the queue_build semantics into the catalogue per DM-stub-registry.md §'Rationale for the fourth entry'. Companion test test_forge_trust_tier_is_core confirms trust_tier == 'core'.
